mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and economic momentum, triggering short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with industrial production growth during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months when CPI exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth directly translates to higher corporate revenue and earnings expectations, driving sustained equity appreciation and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 60-day forward returns with statistical significance across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals a severe supply-side contraction and imminent economic slowdown, triggering defensive portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances following a 1.0 percent monthly industrial production drop.
